'''''Star Wars: Flight of the Falcon''''' was a video game for the [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]], produced by [[THQ]].

==Publisher's summary==
''In ''Star Wars: Flight of the Falcon'', gamers can pilot the ''Millennium Falcon'', the most popular vehicle from the classic ''Star Wars'' universe, in 14 combat-filled missions interweaving the movie storylines with all new plot twists. Featuring highly detailed environments and ships, frantic battles and high-speed flying action, ''Flight of the Falcon'' lets gamers battle the Galactic Empire in the skies of Yavin, Endor and beyond.''

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=FLIGHT OF THE FALCON
|text=As the Galactic Empire
threatens to wipe away the
last remnants of the
Republic, the galaxy stands
poised on the brink of
civil war.

Resistance to the Empire's
tyranny and oppression has
spread, culminating in the
formation of the Rebel
Alliance. However, the
Empire is in the final
stages of preparation to
crush the Rebellion.

The growing storm of events
threatens to engulf the
galaxy, including a Corellian
smuggler piloting the
MILLENNIUM FALCON en route
to Tatooine at the behest
of the crime lord Jabba
the Hutt....}}

==Gameplay==
===Cheat codes===
To enable cheat codes: While playing, press START to pause the game. Then Hold L+R and press Up, Right, A, START, A, Down, A, START, START. The game will unpause. Pause and select Quit. The codes can then be entered under Password.
*'''SPVD''' - Infinite shield
*'''MONG''' - Infinite continues
*'''BLOB''' - Infinite missiles
*'''GOGO''' - Rapid fire (hold A)
*'''FVNK''' - Unlock all levels
*'''82V2''' - Unlock level 1
*'''48TF''' - Unlock level 2
*'''GOON''' - Unlock Credits
*'''F4C3''' - View Team Photo

Packages of ''Star Wars'' PEZ candy contained two secret "Millenni-codes," listed above for unlocking levels 1 and 2.

==Continuity==
The game contradicts [[Star Wars Legends|established continuity]] in several places:
*In the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]] level, the player jumps to hyperspace when the level is complete. In [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the ''Falcon''{{'s}} hyperdrive is still inoperative at this point.
*The next level depicts the player attacking the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], whereas the ''Falcon'' never fought that vessel while on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].
*Finally, the last level in the segment modeled after ''The Empire Strikes Back'' has the player running through a trench on the hull of the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], while the ''Falcon'' never flew through any such trench on the vast warship.
